_comment: Quality-guardrail ratchet baseline. Metrics may only DECREASE without review; an INCREASE fails CI (see ci/check_quality_guardrails.py). #721 (startup.rs extraction) then merged, removing ~227 lines from the bin — bin line ceiling tightened 4186→3959 to lock that gain, and the new SG-008 startup.rs is now tracked (line ceiling + panic_budget 0, keeping the fail-closed predicate panic-free) and added to ownership_scope. WS-0.5 (/metrics, PR #774, reviewed+merged) grew three tracked files with observability code and its tests: the bin gained the LockedOut scrape DoD test (3997→4087 lines; panic budget 117→121 — the four new expect/unwraps are all in that test), verifier.rs gained the AppState fleet_metrics field (1092→1099), policy_layer.rs gained the denial classifier + counting + two test modules (1036→1154). Fix-PR D (#772 F3, audit-durability observability) added the `incident_durability_failures` counter: verifier.rs gained the AppState field + doc (1099→1108), and the bin's LockedOut-scrape DoD test gained one expected-series line (4087→4088). No new panics in either file (the counter field is not a panic site). Fix-PR E (#774 F1, silent scrape resolver) added `resolve_posture_snapshot_silent` to the bin's posture_engine_v2 import, expanding the single-line `use` to three lines (4088→4090); no new panics. (policy_layer.rs shrank — `classify_gate_denial` was deleted in favour of the authoritative `route_command_verdict` — so its ceiling stays.) Ceilings re-pinned at exactly those values — the next growth still fails. When a refactor legitimately raises a ceiling, bump it with a justification in the PR; when it lowers one, tighten it here. G7 key/identity lifecycle (per-principal tokens #802, RBAC #803, attribution #804) grew the bin with the auth + record-admin-action attribution middleware and their tests (4090→4241, no new panics). G7 transport-security enforcement (#805, the mesh-mTLS option) grew the bin with the `require_secure_transport` middleware + the router-level ordering tests (4241→4382; panic 121→126 — the five new expect/unwraps are all in the router test module) and grew verifier.rs with `TransportSecurityConfig` + the pure `request_transport_is_secure` predicate + its unit tests (1108→1233; panic 27→28 — the one new unwrap is a `HeaderValue::from_str` in a test). Auth-middleware extraction (#721 pattern) then moved the auth path — admin-token auth + RBAC, transport identity/security, admin-action attribution, and their G7 tests — out of the bin into the new tracked `auth.rs` submodule: the bin TIGHTENS 4382→4079 lines and 126→121 panics (the five test expect/unwraps moved with the router tests) to lock the gain; `auth.rs` is newly tracked at 328 lines / panic_budget 5 (all five are in the moved router tests — the production auth code is panic-free) and added to ownership_scope. WS-1 UNIFICATION (cherry-pick of the advisor authz branch + retirement of the env registry): the bin grew 4079→4139 (principals mint/list/revoke routes, the carved-out auditor_routes group, scope-layer comments) and auth.rs grew 328→445 with the unified authorize_scope engine + four scope wrappers + AuthenticatedPrincipal (panic 5→7 — both new expect/unwraps are in the added auditor-gate router test). Counterweight: security.rs shrank ~570→181 (the retired env registry); security.rs is not ratchet-tracked, so the net repo-wide LOC change is strongly negative. WS-1 Track 1.2 (opt-in in-process TLS termination, docs/safety/TRANSPORT_SECURITY.md §4) grew the bin 4139→4176 with the pre-bind fail-closed TLS-config resolution + the serve-mode branch (no new panics — both error arms exit(1)) and added the new tracked auth-sibling tls.rs (321 lines / panic_budget 17 — all 17 expect/unwraps are in the #[cfg(test)] module: the resolve_tls truth table, the config-loader cases, and the live-handshake test; the production resolve/load/serve code is panic-free). Copilot #810 hardening: serve_tls bounds each handshake with HANDSHAKE_TIMEOUT and drains in-flight connection tasks via a JoinSet up to DRAIN_GRACE on shutdown (285→321). tls.rs added to ownership_scope. WS-1 Track 1.2 mTLS client-cert → principal: tls.rs grew 321→521 (client-CA WebPkiClientVerifier config + peer_cert_fingerprint extraction/injection + the two live mTLS tests; panic 17→36, all 19 new expect/unwraps in the #[cfg(test)] live-mTLS tests — production code stays panic-free), auth.rs grew 445→478 (the no-bearer cert-fingerprint resolution fallback in authorize_scope; panic unchanged at 7), and the bin grew 4176→4181 (the two admin cert-principal routes). cert_principals store module + the authz cert-path predicate branch are not ratchet-tracked files. Copilot #811 hardening: tls.rs 521→523 (path-generic load_cert_chain error, shared server/CA helper) and auth.rs 478→488 (resolved_via_cert flag → mtls-cert vs api-token credential in the Allow log); no new panics. STOP-GATE REVIEW H3 (HA split-brain fence): policy_layer.rs 1154→1236 — the WriteState/SystemMutation arm of enforce_posture_routing now gates on `mode_active` FIRST via the new pure `mutation_fence_verdict` predicate + `MutationFence` enum (a demoted/standby instance is refused independent of the epoch cache, which is defeated on a disk-wedge self-demotion where cached_db_epoch freezes == held_epoch), plus two exhaustive unit tests over (is_active × held × db). STOP-GATE REVIEW H1 (over-acceleration when steering also clamps): policy_layer.rs 1245→1286 — the frozen kinematics contract gained an `EnforceAction::ClampBoth { linear, steering }` verdict (the `(v_clamped, delta_clamped) == (true, true)` cell previously returned ClampSteering and DROPPED the velocity correction, forwarding the original over-accelerating speed). The deployed consumer gained the matching `EnforcementOutcomeKind::ClampBoth` + `as_str` + `EnforcementOutcome::clamp_both` constructor + the ClampBoth dispatch arm (overwrites BOTH fields). Panic budget unchanged at 14 (the new arm uses `?` + tracing, no new unwrap/expect/panic). The M1 reverse-sign fix + the ClampBoth ripple across the other EnforceAction consumers (kirra-core capture/contract_consumer/kinematics_sim, kirra-trajectory, governor-service, wire-client mirror, parko-kirra diverse) are in non-ratchet-tracked files. STOP-GATE REVIEW H2 (promoted-standby posture freshness): the bin grew 4181→4220 — the Active-mode posture-freshness wiring (posture-engine worker + telemetry watchdog + periodic refresh loop + local-asset feed) was extracted from the inline `if is_active()` startup block into a new shared `wire_active_posture_freshness(&Arc<ServiceState>)` helper (heavily doc-commented) so the standby→Active promotion path can (re)start those tasks too via an on-promote hook threaded into `spawn_promotion_monitor`; without it a runtime-promoted node's cache went stale one TTL after promotion and every gated route fail-closed. Net +39 lines is the helper's doc block minus the now-collapsed startup block; panic budget unchanged at 121 (the helper uses tracing + `std::process::exit` on the fail-closed double-set, no new unwrap/expect/panic). Panic budget unchanged at 14 (the predicate is panic-free; the tests use assert_eq). Copilot #816 follow-up: the two hand-picked mutation_fence tests were consolidated into one loop-driven test that drives the predicate over the FULL finite domain {active,standby}×{0,1,2}² (18 cases), each case's expected verdict computed independently from the H3 spec — making the doc/test "exhaustive" claim literally true rather than softening the wording (1236→1245, +9 lines; panic budget unchanged at 14 — the loop uses assert_eq, no new panic site). WS-4 / TRACK 3 (Fleet Plane — OTA governor-artifact campaign control plane): the bin grew 4220→4430 with the campaign route mounts (create/list/get/arm/advance/halt on admin_routes) + the four real-router campaign tests (fail-closed-without-credential, the end-to-end lifecycle + fail-closed posture-halt, the cold-posture halt, and the validation/error-mapping test) and their `campaign_router`/`campaign_req` helpers; panic budget 121→125 — the four new expect/unwraps are all in the `campaign_req` helper + the halt test's posture-cache write, i.e. #[cfg(test)] only (the campaign handlers are panic-free). The pure engine (`src/ota_campaign.rs`), its store module (`src/verifier_store/ota_campaigns.rs`) and the handler submodule (`src/bin/kirra_verifier_service/campaigns.rs`, State<Arc<ServiceState>> throughout) are not line/panic ratchet-tracked; campaigns.rs is added to ownership_scope. WS-4 / TRACK 3 follow-up (OTA campaign posture-sweep monitor): the bin grew 4430→4445 — `wire_active_posture_freshness` now also spawns the `campaign_monitor` background sweep (so a promoted standby runs it too), auto-halting active campaigns on a CONFIRMED fleet regression between explicit advances; no new panics (the spawn block is tracing + Arc clones). The monitor engine (`src/campaign_monitor.rs`) + the `load_active_campaigns` store query are in non-ratchet-tracked files. WS-4 / TRACK 3 follow-up (node artifact-assignment query): the bin grew 4445→4544 — the public read-only `/fleet/campaigns/assignment/{node_id}` mount plus the four real-router tests (Nominal resolve + cohort miss, LockedOut posture-gating denial) and their `seed_rolling_campaign`/`assignment_req` helpers; panic budget 125→131 — the six new expect/unwraps are all in those #[cfg(test)] helpers (the handler is panic-free). The engine resolver (`resolve_node_assignment`/`is_node_rolled` in `src/ota_campaign.rs`) and the assignment handler (in the already-tracked `fleet.rs`, State<Arc<ServiceState>>) carry the logic; the handler is a GET (classifies ReadTelemetry, posture-gated, no classifier change). Copilot #829 follow-up: the assignment router test now seeds a REACHABLE Rolling@50% campaign (arm + one real advance, not a manufactured Rolling@100 the engine never produces) and asserts real partial-rollout membership end-to-end via deterministically-chosen rolled/unrolled nodes (`node_rolled_at_seed`) — a strictly stronger test (4544→4575 lines; panic 131→133 — the two new expect/unwraps are the seed's `advance().unwrap()` and `node_rolled_at_seed`'s `.expect`, both #[cfg(test)]). The 500 body was also made specific ("failed to load active campaigns"). WS-4 / TRACK 3 (WORM off-box audit shipper scheduler): the bin grew 4575→4587 — `wire_active_posture_freshness` now also spawns the env-gated (`KIRRA_AUDIT_SHIP_PATH`) background audit shipper on the Active instance, appending new hash-chained audit records off-box each `AUDIT_SHIP_INTERVAL_MS`; no new panics (the spawn block is an `if` + tracing + Arc clone). The shipper engine (`spawn_audit_shipper`/`ship_and_advance` in `src/audit_shipper.rs`) is a non-ratchet-tracked file. WS-4 / TRACK 3 (campaign fleet observability): the bin grew 4587→4651 — the admin `GET /system/campaigns/summary` route mount (registered before `{campaign_id}` so the static segment wins the match) plus two real-router tests (the summary reflecting counts/active-progress/halted-reason end-to-end, and the static-vs-param non-shadowing proof); panic budget unchanged at 133 (the summary handler is panic-free, and the tests use serde_json indexing + assert_eq, no new expect/unwrap). The pure `summarize_campaigns` + `CampaignSummary`/`CampaignProgress`/`HaltedCampaign` (in `src/ota_campaign.rs`) and the `campaigns_summary_handler` (in the ownership-tracked `campaigns.rs`, State<Arc<ServiceState>>, read-replica) are not line/panic ratchet-tracked. WS-4 / TRACK 3 (node adoption reporting): the bin grew 4651→4705 — the identity-gated `POST /fleet/campaigns/report` mount (in the Tier-1 identity_gated_routes group + the bare test-router copy) plus the end-to-end adoption test (report → summary `applied_nodes` reflects it, bad-digest 400, upsert non-double-count) and the report route added to the WS-1 scope-gated fail-closed enumeration; panic budget unchanged at 133 (the report handler is panic-free and the tests use serde_json indexing + assert_eq). The pure `NodeArtifactStatus` + the `applied_nodes` adoption join in `summarize_campaigns` (`src/ota_campaign.rs`), the `report_node_artifact` handler (in the already-tracked `fleet.rs`, State<Arc<ServiceState>>, identity-gated — a node write needs a credential), and the `node_artifact_status` table + `upsert_node_artifact_status`/`load_node_artifact_statuses` (non-audit-chained observability upsert in `verifier_store/ota_campaigns.rs`) carry the logic; none are line/panic ratchet-tracked beyond the bin's route mounts. WS-4 / TRACK 3 (console rollout view): the bin grew 4705→4754 — the `/console/campaigns` route mount in the public read-only console_routes group + the console test seeding a reachable Rolling@10% campaign and two adoption reports through the store, then asserting the view's rollout progress + `applied_nodes`; panic budget 133→138 — the five new expect/unwraps are all in that #[cfg(test)] seed (Campaign::new/arm/advance + the two store.call result layers). The `console_campaigns` handler (in the ownership-tracked `console.rs`, State<Arc<ServiceState>>, read-replica, reusing `summarize_campaigns`) is not line/panic ratchet-tracked; the SPA section is `static/console.html` (not Rust). WS-4 / TRACK 3 (campaign metrics on /metrics): the bin grew 4754→4757 — the LockedOut /metrics scrape DoD test now also asserts the `kirra_ota_campaigns_total{` family survives LockedOut; no new panics. The pure `campaign_metrics_prometheus` formatter (in `src/ota_campaign.rs`) + the replica-read append in `metrics_endpoint` (in the already-tracked-for-ownership `fleet.rs`, posture-exempt) are not line/panic ratchet-tracked. WS-4 / TRACK 3 (attestation-signed adoption reports): the bin grew 4757→4829 / panic 138→142 — the signed-report integration test in attestation_nonce_handler_tests (valid Ed25519 sig → stored attested, forged sig → 401 fail-closed, unsigned → accepted-but-unattested) plus the console test's NodeArtifactStatus literal gaining `attested: false`; the four new expect/unwraps are all in that #[cfg(test)] test (the attested_of read + serde_json::from_value + B64.decode). The `adoption_report_signing_payload` + domain (in `src/attestation.rs`), the optional-signature verification branch in the `report_node_artifact` handler (in `fleet.rs`), the `attested` column + monotonic upsert (in `verifier_store/ota_campaigns.rs`), and the `attested_nodes` summary/metric field (in `src/ota_campaign.rs`) are not line/panic ratchet-tracked. Copilot #840 review: the signed-report test gained a far-future-timestamp → 400 case (bin 4829→4850, no new panics — serde_json + assert_eq); the bounded future-skew guard in `report_node_artifact` (`fleet.rs`), the `attested`-monotonic-per-digest upsert (`verifier_store/ota_campaigns.rs`), and the O(nodes+campaigns) adoption pre-index in `summarize_campaigns` (`src/ota_campaign.rs`) are in non-ratchet-tracked files. WP-03 (MGA G-10, control-plane backpressure): the bin grew 4850→4875 — the two-pool wiring block in build_app (API vs console isolated concurrency pools + body cap, probes exempt) plus the module declaration; panic budget unchanged at 142 (the wiring is tracing + env_limit_or, which exits(1) fail-closed rather than panicking). The stack itself (load-shed → 429+Retry-After, GlobalConcurrencyLimit, DefaultBodyLimit) lives in the new tracked backpressure.rs (187 lines / panic_budget 11 — all 11 unwraps are in the #[cfg(test)] shed/recovery/body-cap tests; the production stack is panic-free) and is added to ownership_scope. WP-05 (MGA G-10, observability): the bin grew 4875→4890 (the observability module declaration + the outermost request-observability layer in build_app, with the posture-gate comment retitled 'Outermost GATE' — the new layer makes no admission decisions); policy_layer.rs grew 1286→1305 (the EnvelopeLatencyGuard drop-guard recording deployed-path actuator-envelope latency into the new FleetSafetyMetrics histogram — no new panic sites, budget unchanged at 14). The middleware itself (request id mint/validation + tracing span + http latency histogram) lives in the new tracked observability.rs (≤185 lines / panic_budget 12 — all 12 unwrap/expect tokens are in the #[cfg(test)] module; the production middleware is panic-free and falls back to dropping the header rather than panicking) and is added to ownership_scope. The LatencyHistogram type + the two FleetSafetyMetrics histogram fields live in src/metrics.rs (not ratchet-tracked). Copilot #854 review: backpressure.rs 187→191 (module-doc corrections only — the actual layer order incl. DefaultBodyLimit's outermost-but-inert position, and posture-gate wording tightened to 'outermost gate' vs the WP-05 observability outermost layer); LatencyHistogram record order flipped count-first/bucket-last so a concurrent scrape can never observe a finite bucket exceeding +Inf/_count (metrics.rs, not ratchet-tracked); no new panics anywhere. WP-09 (S-DG1 divergence→posture): verifier.rs grew 1233→1253 — the three AppState fields (divergence_degraded_active / divergence_lockout_active sticky flag / divergence_recovery_streak) + their S-DG1 doc comments and Default init; no new panic sites (budget unchanged at 28). The apply/composition logic lives in posture_engine[_v2].rs (not line-ratchet-tracked); the parko-side PostureSignalSink + PostureEngineSenderSink are parko-kirra (own workspace). WP-15 (MGA G-19, mTLS cert lifecycle): auth.rs grew 488→506 — the mTLS cert-resolution branch now folds the pinned cert's X.509 expiry (`is_expired(now)`) into the same invalid-credential flag the pure predicate denies on, and WARN-logs an expired-but-not-revoked cert distinctly (panic budget unchanged at 7 — the added lines are a `now_ms()` read + `unwrap_or(0)` in the log field + the ResolvedPrincipal construction, no new unwrap/expect/panic). The bin grew 4890→4902 — the Active-block spawn of the new `cert_expiry_monitor` background sweep + its startup log (tracing + Arc clone, no new panics). The store cert-lifecycle (`CertPrincipalRecord`/`CertExpirySummary`/`cert_expiry_summary` + the `not_after_ms` column & migration in `verifier_store/{mod,cert_principals}.rs`), the monitor engine (`src/cert_expiry_monitor.rs`), the `cert_expiry_prometheus` formatter (`src/metrics.rs`), and the register/list handlers (`principals.rs`, State<Arc<ServiceState>>) are not line/panic ratchet-tracked; cert_expiry_monitor.rs is a new module.
